programme.title: Awakening Lion-3
programme.desc: Xingshi, or literally “awakened lion,” is a name for the southern style of the lion dance. Historically, it evolved from a court dance in the Tang Dynasty almost 1,200 years ago. With the southward migration of the population from the central plains, lion dance culture was introduced to the south. In the Ming Dynasty around 400 years ago, xingshi appeared in Guangdong and was then widely spread throughout southern China and even overseas. Lion dance has become a very important part of Chinese culture.

programme.title: INVESTING IN JOY: Inside China's new emotional economy
programme.desc: Today's young Chinese generation are driving a new \"emotional economy,\" spending not just on necessities but on feelings. From trendy toys that offer joy and community, to serene scents that provide a moment of peace – they are investing in personal well-being, reshaping the consumer landscape.

programme.title: Rhythms of the Forest: A Sound of Li Instruments
programme.desc: Li bamboo and wood instruments offer a journey into the unique sounds of Hainan's Li ethnic group. Explore the ancient craftsmanship and musical heritage, from the resonant tones of bamboo flutes to the percussive rhythms of wooden drums. Experience the natural melodies that tell the stories of the Li people.

programme.title: The Inner Horizon: China's extreme sports revolution
programme.desc: They say in China, the most exciting frontiers aren't always on a map. A new cultural wave is sweeping the nation, turning vertical rock faces, open skies, and dense forests into new playgrounds. What was once the domain of a handful of adventurers is now a booming lifestyle for millions of young Chinese. I recently ventured out to explore why extreme sports are becoming the new national pastime, and what this reveals about the changing spirit of China.

programme.title: Forged in Legend: A Glimpse of Longquan Blades
programme.desc: Longquan swords, legendary blades from China, embody centuries of masterful craftsmanship. Forged with unique folding techniques and a distinctive star-patterned finish, these swords are renowned for their sharpness, resilience, and artistic value. Discover the rich history and cultural significance of these revered symbols of Chinese heritage.

programme.title: The beat of the city: Inside China's booming Livehouse scene
programme.desc: From the intimate jazz vibes of Beijing's Blue Note to the electric energy of a Guangzhou livehouse — we dive into China's booming nightlife scene. Discover how these venues are more than just places for a night out — they're cultural hubs where young audiences connect, artists innovate, and the city's heart beats to a brand new rhythm.

programme.title: Women of the Xiangjiang River Ep.1
programme.desc: The Xiangjiang River runs 800 kilometers through the whole of Hunan Province. Hailed as the mother river of Hunan, it serves as a vital waterway connecting the Yangtze and Pearl River systems. Its source is located in Yongzhou, a land of abundant water resources, rich natural treasures and outstanding people. In this episode, we focus on three young women from Yongzhou: Pan Haiying, a dance teacher; Huang Cuilan, an inheritor of brocade weaving; and Hu Xin, a custodian of Nüshu culture. We document snippets of their daily lives and work, to reflect the profound cultural heritage along the Xiangjiang River and the value pursuits of the women living on its banks.

programme.title: Dive into the world of foreign talent in China's movie industry
programme.desc: We go behind the scenes of China's fast-evolving film industry with foreign actors making their mark here. From script study to on-set cultural moments, they share how they learn, interpret, and help bring Chinese stories to life. Their journeys highlight not only China's growing appeal and openness but also the vibrant, two-way cultural exchange unfolding on the global stage.

programme.title: Women of the Xiangjiang River Ep.2
programme.desc: In this episode, we continue our journey along the Xiangjiang River in Hunan Province, to meet more outstanding women from the cities of Hengyang and Zhuzhou. By documenting snippets of the daily lives and work of a paper-cutting artist, the director of the Hengyang Huagu Opera Troupe, and an inheritor of Liling porcelain craftsmanship, we gain vivid insights into the distinctive cultural traditions of this region.

programme.title: Personalized tours draw more global travelers to Beijing
programme.desc: As China’s visa-free policy continues to attract visitors from around the world, more international tourists are choosing customized experiences in Beijing. Local guides fluent in multiple languages are helping them discover the city in their own way. Reporter Li Qiong joined oneone of the capital’s most popular guides to see how personalized tours are reshaping travel in China.

programme.title: Women of the Xiangjiang River Ep.3
programme.desc: In this episode, we continue our journey along the Xiangjiang River in Hunan Province, traveling to the cities of Zhuzhou and Xiangtan to meet another group of extraordinary women. By capturing snippets of the lives and work of an electric locomotive designer, a flight instructor, a painter, and a poverty alleviation volunteer, we showcase the unique cultural and humanistic charm of this region.

programme.title: From plush toys to holograms: The growing world of AI companionship
programme.desc: Artificial intelligence is evolving—from answering our questions to keeping us company. From robotic pets in our neighborhoods to holographic characters that sense our mood, AI companionship is stepping out of science fiction and into everyday life. What does this mean for human relationships and the future of connection itself? Reporter Zhang Hong explores the rise of these new companions.

programme.title: Women of the Xiangjiang River Ep.4
programme.desc: In this episode, we arrive in Changsha — the economic and cultural hub of Hunan Province, and a core city in the Yangtze River Middle Reaches Economic Zone. Our lens turns to the \"Xiangjiang Ladies\" who work in Changsha’s high-tech and equipment manufacturing sectors. Their daily lives and professional endeavors reflect the city’s innovative vitality, while their after-work gatherings also showcase the dazzling night views along both banks of the Xiangjiang River, highlighting Changsha’s distinct charm as a vibrant capital of leisure and entertainment.

programme.title: Young Chinese dare to express their culture
programme.desc: A fresh chapter in cultural storytelling is being written by young Chinese around the world. Young Chinese are amplifying tradition through trend, donning hanfu not only in historical lanes but in everyday life, and weaving those visuals into the fabric of social media. Our reporter Ioana Gomoi meets the creators who are turning heritage into a trendy cultural inspiration.

programme.title: Indigo Dreams: A Hue of Dali Tie-dye
programme.desc: Dali Tie-dye is the unique textile art of the Bai people, renowned for its exquisite hand-tying and traditional indigo dyeing techniques that create brilliant, one-of-a-kind patterns. Explore the cultural heritage and enduring beauty of this ancient craft from Yunnan.

programme.title: LIVING THE NEW WAVE China's livestream shopping boom redefines e-commerce
programme.desc: Now, we’re taking you inside one of China’s most dynamic phenomena—the livestream shopping revolution. From the misty tea mountains of Guizhou to Beijing’s glittering jewelry studios, millions tune in daily not just to purchase, but to connect. How did this fusion of entertainment and e-commerce become such a powerful cultural force? And what does it tell us about the future of shopping worldwide? Our reporter Lucy Lyv is here with us.

programme.title: Awakening Lion-1
programme.desc: Xingshi, or literally “awakened lion,” is a name for the southern style of the lion dance. Historically, it evolved from a court dance in the Tang Dynasty almost 1,200 years ago. With the southward migration of the population from the central plains, lion dance culture was introduced to the south. In the Ming Dynasty around 400 years ago, xingshi appeared in Guangdong and was then widely spread throughout southern China and even overseas. Lion dance has become a very important part of Chinese culture.

programme.title: Awakening Lion-2
programme.desc: Xingshi, or literally “awakened lion,” is a name for the southern style of the lion dance. Historically, it evolved from a court dance in the Tang Dynasty almost 1,200 years ago. With the southward migration of the population from the central plains, lion dance culture was introduced to the south. In the Ming Dynasty around 400 years ago, xingshi appeared in Guangdong and was then widely spread throughout southern China and even overseas. Lion dance has become a very important part of Chinese culture.

programme.title: Awakening Lion-3
programme.desc: Xingshi, or literally “awakened lion,” is a name for the southern style of the lion dance. Historically, it evolved from a court dance in the Tang Dynasty almost 1,200 years ago. With the southward migration of the population from the central plains, lion dance culture was introduced to the south. In the Ming Dynasty around 400 years ago, xingshi appeared in Guangdong and was then widely spread throughout southern China and even overseas. Lion dance has become a very important part of Chinese culture.

programme.title: INVESTING IN JOY: Inside China's new emotional economy
programme.desc: Today's young Chinese generation are driving a new \"emotional economy,\" spending not just on necessities but on feelings. From trendy toys that offer joy and community, to serene scents that provide a moment of peace – they are investing in personal well-being, reshaping the consumer landscape.

programme.title: Rhythms of the Forest: A Sound of Li Instruments
programme.desc: Li bamboo and wood instruments offer a journey into the unique sounds of Hainan's Li ethnic group. Explore the ancient craftsmanship and musical heritage, from the resonant tones of bamboo flutes to the percussive rhythms of wooden drums. Experience the natural melodies that tell the stories of the Li people.

programme.title: The Inner Horizon: China's extreme sports revolution
programme.desc: They say in China, the most exciting frontiers aren't always on a map. A new cultural wave is sweeping the nation, turning vertical rock faces, open skies, and dense forests into new playgrounds. What was once the domain of a handful of adventurers is now a booming lifestyle for millions of young Chinese. I recently ventured out to explore why extreme sports are becoming the new national pastime, and what this reveals about the changing spirit of China.

programme.title: Forged in Legend: A Glimpse of Longquan Blades
programme.desc: Longquan swords, legendary blades from China, embody centuries of masterful craftsmanship. Forged with unique folding techniques and a distinctive star-patterned finish, these swords are renowned for their sharpness, resilience, and artistic value. Discover the rich history and cultural significance of these revered symbols of Chinese heritage.

programme.title: The beat of the city: Inside China's booming Livehouse scene
programme.desc: From the intimate jazz vibes of Beijing's Blue Note to the electric energy of a Guangzhou livehouse — we dive into China's booming nightlife scene. Discover how these venues are more than just places for a night out — they're cultural hubs where young audiences connect, artists innovate, and the city's heart beats to a brand new rhythm.

programme.title: Women of the Xiangjiang River Ep.1
programme.desc: The Xiangjiang River runs 800 kilometers through the whole of Hunan Province. Hailed as the mother river of Hunan, it serves as a vital waterway connecting the Yangtze and Pearl River systems. Its source is located in Yongzhou, a land of abundant water resources, rich natural treasures and outstanding people. In this episode, we focus on three young women from Yongzhou: Pan Haiying, a dance teacher; Huang Cuilan, an inheritor of brocade weaving; and Hu Xin, a custodian of Nüshu culture. We document snippets of their daily lives and work, to reflect the profound cultural heritage along the Xiangjiang River and the value pursuits of the women living on its banks.

programme.title: Dive into the world of foreign talent in China's movie industry
programme.desc: We go behind the scenes of China's fast-evolving film industry with foreign actors making their mark here. From script study to on-set cultural moments, they share how they learn, interpret, and help bring Chinese stories to life. Their journeys highlight not only China's growing appeal and openness but also the vibrant, two-way cultural exchange unfolding on the global stage.

programme.title: Women of the Xiangjiang River Ep.2
programme.desc: In this episode, we continue our journey along the Xiangjiang River in Hunan Province, to meet more outstanding women from the cities of Hengyang and Zhuzhou. By documenting snippets of the daily lives and work of a paper-cutting artist, the director of the Hengyang Huagu Opera Troupe, and an inheritor of Liling porcelain craftsmanship, we gain vivid insights into the distinctive cultural traditions of this region.

programme.title: Personalized tours draw more global travelers to Beijing
programme.desc: As China’s visa-free policy continues to attract visitors from around the world, more international tourists are choosing customized experiences in Beijing. Local guides fluent in multiple languages are helping them discover the city in their own way. Reporter Li Qiong joined oneone of the capital’s most popular guides to see how personalized tours are reshaping travel in China.

programme.title: Women of the Xiangjiang River Ep.3
programme.desc: In this episode, we continue our journey along the Xiangjiang River in Hunan Province, traveling to the cities of Zhuzhou and Xiangtan to meet another group of extraordinary women. By capturing snippets of the lives and work of an electric locomotive designer, a flight instructor, a painter, and a poverty alleviation volunteer, we showcase the unique cultural and humanistic charm of this region.

programme.title: From plush toys to holograms: The growing world of AI companionship
programme.desc: Artificial intelligence is evolving—from answering our questions to keeping us company. From robotic pets in our neighborhoods to holographic characters that sense our mood, AI companionship is stepping out of science fiction and into everyday life. What does this mean for human relationships and the future of connection itself? Reporter Zhang Hong explores the rise of these new companions.

programme.title: Women of the Xiangjiang River Ep.4
programme.desc: In this episode, we arrive in Changsha — the economic and cultural hub of Hunan Province, and a core city in the Yangtze River Middle Reaches Economic Zone. Our lens turns to the \"Xiangjiang Ladies\" who work in Changsha’s high-tech and equipment manufacturing sectors. Their daily lives and professional endeavors reflect the city’s innovative vitality, while their after-work gatherings also showcase the dazzling night views along both banks of the Xiangjiang River, highlighting Changsha’s distinct charm as a vibrant capital of leisure and entertainment.

programme.title: Young Chinese dare to express their culture
programme.desc: A fresh chapter in cultural storytelling is being written by young Chinese around the world. Young Chinese are amplifying tradition through trend, donning hanfu not only in historical lanes but in everyday life, and weaving those visuals into the fabric of social media. Our reporter Ioana Gomoi meets the creators who are turning heritage into a trendy cultural inspiration.

programme.title: Indigo Dreams: A Hue of Dali Tie-dye
programme.desc: Dali Tie-dye is the unique textile art of the Bai people, renowned for its exquisite hand-tying and traditional indigo dyeing techniques that create brilliant, one-of-a-kind patterns. Explore the cultural heritage and enduring beauty of this ancient craft from Yunnan.

programme.title: LIVING THE NEW WAVE China's livestream shopping boom redefines e-commerce
programme.desc: Now, we’re taking you inside one of China’s most dynamic phenomena—the livestream shopping revolution. From the misty tea mountains of Guizhou to Beijing’s glittering jewelry studios, millions tune in daily not just to purchase, but to connect. How did this fusion of entertainment and e-commerce become such a powerful cultural force? And what does it tell us about the future of shopping worldwide? Our reporter Lucy Lyv is here with us.

programme.title: Awakening Lion-1
programme.desc: Xingshi, or literally “awakened lion,” is a name for the southern style of the lion dance. Historically, it evolved from a court dance in the Tang Dynasty almost 1,200 years ago. With the southward migration of the population from the central plains, lion dance culture was introduced to the south. In the Ming Dynasty around 400 years ago, xingshi appeared in Guangdong and was then widely spread throughout southern China and even overseas. Lion dance has become a very important part of Chinese culture.

programme.title: Awakening Lion-2
programme.desc: Xingshi, or literally “awakened lion,” is a name for the southern style of the lion dance. Historically, it evolved from a court dance in the Tang Dynasty almost 1,200 years ago. With the southward migration of the population from the central plains, lion dance culture was introduced to the south. In the Ming Dynasty around 400 years ago, xingshi appeared in Guangdong and was then widely spread throughout southern China and even overseas. Lion dance has become a very important part of Chinese culture.

programme.title: Awakening Lion-3
programme.desc: Xingshi, or literally “awakened lion,” is a name for the southern style of the lion dance. Historically, it evolved from a court dance in the Tang Dynasty almost 1,200 years ago. With the southward migration of the population from the central plains, lion dance culture was introduced to the south. In the Ming Dynasty around 400 years ago, xingshi appeared in Guangdong and was then widely spread throughout southern China and even overseas. Lion dance has become a very important part of Chinese culture.

programme.title: INVESTING IN JOY: Inside China's new emotional economy
programme.desc: Today's young Chinese generation are driving a new \"emotional economy,\" spending not just on necessities but on feelings. From trendy toys that offer joy and community, to serene scents that provide a moment of peace – they are investing in personal well-being, reshaping the consumer landscape.

programme.title: Rhythms of the Forest: A Sound of Li Instruments
programme.desc: Li bamboo and wood instruments offer a journey into the unique sounds of Hainan's Li ethnic group. Explore the ancient craftsmanship and musical heritage, from the resonant tones of bamboo flutes to the percussive rhythms of wooden drums. Experience the natural melodies that tell the stories of the Li people.

programme.title: The Inner Horizon: China's extreme sports revolution
programme.desc: They say in China, the most exciting frontiers aren't always on a map. A new cultural wave is sweeping the nation, turning vertical rock faces, open skies, and dense forests into new playgrounds. What was once the domain of a handful of adventurers is now a booming lifestyle for millions of young Chinese. I recently ventured out to explore why extreme sports are becoming the new national pastime, and what this reveals about the changing spirit of China.

programme.title: Forged in Legend: A Glimpse of Longquan Blades
programme.desc: Longquan swords, legendary blades from China, embody centuries of masterful craftsmanship. Forged with unique folding techniques and a distinctive star-patterned finish, these swords are renowned for their sharpness, resilience, and artistic value. Discover the rich history and cultural significance of these revered symbols of Chinese heritage.

programme.title: The beat of the city: Inside China's booming Livehouse scene
programme.desc: From the intimate jazz vibes of Beijing's Blue Note to the electric energy of a Guangzhou livehouse — we dive into China's booming nightlife scene. Discover how these venues are more than just places for a night out — they're cultural hubs where young audiences connect, artists innovate, and the city's heart beats to a brand new rhythm.

programme.title: All About the Yak
programme.desc: In this episode, we go to Nangqian, Yushu, Qinghai Province, to get close contact with the Yak in Tibet, which is known as the \" Vehicle on the Plateau\". During the bustling yak culture festival, people gather from near and far, meadows are crowded, and yak appraisal activities are also in full swing. As the journey continues, we are able to understand several aspects of the yak culture which is closely related to the life of Tibetan people.

programme.title: Dive into the world of foreign talent in China's movie industry
programme.desc: We go behind the scenes of China's fast-evolving film industry with foreign actors making their mark here. From script study to on-set cultural moments, they share how they learn, interpret, and help bring Chinese stories to life. Their journeys highlight not only China's growing appeal and openness but also the vibrant, two-way cultural exchange unfolding on the global stage.

programme.title: The Yak and the Nomad
programme.desc: Qinghai’s Yushu Prefecture is one of China’s key yak-producing regions. This trip took us to Nangqian County, where we explored the yak industry that lies at the heart of Tibetan herders’ daily lives. Trekking into the far-flung pastoral corners of the plateau, we listened closely to the stories behind the hurdles and hopes of pastoral life and development.

programme.title: Personalized tours draw more global travelers to Beijing
programme.desc: As China’s visa-free policy continues to attract visitors from around the world, more international tourists are choosing customized experiences in Beijing. Local guides fluent in multiple languages are helping them discover the city in their own way. Reporter Li Qiong joined oneone of the capital’s most popular guides to see how personalized tours are reshaping travel in China.

programme.title: China Bound: Tianjin
programme.desc: Tianran He explores the vibrant port city of Tianjin. Strolling through its historic quarters, he discovers how old buildings are being preserved and revitalized, and savors the rich flavors of authentic Tianjin cuisine. To work off the calories, he joins energetic retirees for a morning workout in the park, then takes in the lively nightlife along the banks of the Haihe River. From folk handicrafts on Ancient Culture Street to the National Maritime Museum and an aircraft carrier theme park, Tianran discovers that Tianjin is a truly multifacted city.

programme.title: From plush toys to holograms: The growing world of AI companionship
programme.desc: Artificial intelligence is evolving—from answering our questions to keeping us company. From robotic pets in our neighborhoods to holographic characters that sense our mood, AI companionship is stepping out of science fiction and into everyday life. What does this mean for human relationships and the future of connection itself? Reporter Zhang Hong explores the rise of these new companions.

programme.title: China Highly Recommended: Changsha
programme.desc: Episode 1 of Travelogue's \"China Highly Recommended\" tells the story of Changsha, one of China's happiest cities. Following the lives of four of its foreign residents: A French executive chef, a Japanese podcaster, and two American teachers, we discover the reasons why they made Changsha their second home, and explore the city with them as only locals do. This episode will be released as a 30 minute travel documentary to be broadcast on CGTN's television channel and social media platform, along with a series of video clips and photos.

programme.title: Young Chinese dare to express their culture
programme.desc: A fresh chapter in cultural storytelling is being written by young Chinese around the world. Young Chinese are amplifying tradition through trend, donning hanfu not only in historical lanes but in everyday life, and weaving those visuals into the fabric of social media. Our reporter Ioana Gomoi meets the creators who are turning heritage into a trendy cultural inspiration.

programme.title: Tales in Light and Shadow
programme.desc: Beijing shadow puppets bring ancient Chinese tales to life with intricately crafted leather figures. Behind an illuminated screen, these colorful silhouettes dance, enacting epic stories and folklore accompanied by traditional music. Discover this captivating performing art, a cherished part of China's rich cultural tapestry and a mesmerizing visual spectacle.

programme.title: LIVING THE NEW WAVE China's livestream shopping boom redefines e-commerce
programme.desc: Now, we’re taking you inside one of China’s most dynamic phenomena—the livestream shopping revolution. From the misty tea mountains of Guizhou to Beijing’s glittering jewelry studios, millions tune in daily not just to purchase, but to connect. How did this fusion of entertainment and e-commerce become such a powerful cultural force? And what does it tell us about the future of shopping worldwide? Our reporter Lucy Lyv is here with us.

programme.title: Taohuawu New Year's Painting
programme.desc: Taohuawu New Year woodblock prints, originating from Suzhou, are iconic symbols of Chinese folk art. These vibrant prints, traditionally displayed during Chinese New Year, depict auspicious scenes, deities, and daily life, embodying wishes for prosperity and happiness. Explore the intricate carving and printing techniques that bring these colorful cultural treasures to life.

programme.title: INVESTING IN JOY: Inside China's new emotional economy
programme.desc: Today's young Chinese generation are driving a new \"emotional economy,\" spending not just on necessities but on feelings. From trendy toys that offer joy and community, to serene scents that provide a moment of peace – they are investing in personal well-being, reshaping the consumer landscape.

programme.title: A Sip of Mount Wuyi
programme.desc: Wuyi rock tea, nurtured in the mineral-rich cliffs of the Wuyi Mountains, is a distinctive oolong embodying a unique \"rock rhyme\" (yan yun)—a taste of nature's harmony. More than a beverage, teas like Da Hong Pao invite mindful appreciation, reflecting centuries of tradition and a philosophy of patience in their complex roasted notes and enduring aftertaste. Discover the terroir, meticulous processing, and the spirit of Chinese tea culture within these exceptional leaves.

programme.title: The Inner Horizon: China's extreme sports revolution
programme.desc: They say in China, the most exciting frontiers aren't always on a map. A new cultural wave is sweeping the nation, turning vertical rock faces, open skies, and dense forests into new playgrounds. What was once the domain of a handful of adventurers is now a booming lifestyle for millions of young Chinese. I recently ventured out to explore why extreme sports are becoming the new national pastime, and what this reveals about the changing spirit of China.

programme.title: A Lifelong Journey of Xuan Paper
programme.desc: The host travels to Jingxian, Anhui—known as the hometown of Xuan paper—to experience the intricate process of making this traditional paper. Along the way, they uncover the secrets behind its thousand-year legacy and witness the craftsmanship of Xuan paper inheritors at every stage. Through vivid, personal stories, the episode reveals the hidden struggles and quiet perseverance that have kept this intangible cultural heritage alive across generations.

programme.title: The beat of the city: Inside China's booming Livehouse scene
programme.desc: From the intimate jazz vibes of Beijing's Blue Note to the electric energy of a Guangzhou livehouse — we dive into China's booming nightlife scene. Discover how these venues are more than just places for a night out — they're cultural hubs where young audiences connect, artists innovate, and the city's heart beats to a brand new rhythm.

programme.title: All About the Yak
programme.desc: In this episode, we go to Nangqian, Yushu, Qinghai Province, to get close contact with the Yak in Tibet, which is known as the \" Vehicle on the Plateau\". During the bustling yak culture festival, people gather from near and far, meadows are crowded, and yak appraisal activities are also in full swing. As the journey continues, we are able to understand several aspects of the yak culture which is closely related to the life of Tibetan people.

programme.title: Dive into the world of foreign talent in China's movie industry
programme.desc: We go behind the scenes of China's fast-evolving film industry with foreign actors making their mark here. From script study to on-set cultural moments, they share how they learn, interpret, and help bring Chinese stories to life. Their journeys highlight not only China's growing appeal and openness but also the vibrant, two-way cultural exchange unfolding on the global stage.

programme.title: The Yak and the Nomad
programme.desc: Qinghai’s Yushu Prefecture is one of China’s key yak-producing regions. This trip took us to Nangqian County, where we explored the yak industry that lies at the heart of Tibetan herders’ daily lives. Trekking into the far-flung pastoral corners of the plateau, we listened closely to the stories behind the hurdles and hopes of pastoral life and development.

programme.title: Personalized tours draw more global travelers to Beijing
programme.desc: As China’s visa-free policy continues to attract visitors from around the world, more international tourists are choosing customized experiences in Beijing. Local guides fluent in multiple languages are helping them discover the city in their own way. Reporter Li Qiong joined oneone of the capital’s most popular guides to see how personalized tours are reshaping travel in China.

programme.title: China Bound: Tianjin
programme.desc: Tianran He explores the vibrant port city of Tianjin. Strolling through its historic quarters, he discovers how old buildings are being preserved and revitalized, and savors the rich flavors of authentic Tianjin cuisine. To work off the calories, he joins energetic retirees for a morning workout in the park, then takes in the lively nightlife along the banks of the Haihe River. From folk handicrafts on Ancient Culture Street to the National Maritime Museum and an aircraft carrier theme park, Tianran discovers that Tianjin is a truly multifacted city.

programme.title: From plush toys to holograms: The growing world of AI companionship
programme.desc: Artificial intelligence is evolving—from answering our questions to keeping us company. From robotic pets in our neighborhoods to holographic characters that sense our mood, AI companionship is stepping out of science fiction and into everyday life. What does this mean for human relationships and the future of connection itself? Reporter Zhang Hong explores the rise of these new companions.

programme.title: China Highly Recommended: Changsha
programme.desc: Episode 1 of Travelogue's \"China Highly Recommended\" tells the story of Changsha, one of China's happiest cities. Following the lives of four of its foreign residents: A French executive chef, a Japanese podcaster, and two American teachers, we discover the reasons why they made Changsha their second home, and explore the city with them as only locals do. This episode will be released as a 30 minute travel documentary to be broadcast on CGTN's television channel and social media platform, along with a series of video clips and photos.

programme.title: Young Chinese dare to express their culture
programme.desc: A fresh chapter in cultural storytelling is being written by young Chinese around the world. Young Chinese are amplifying tradition through trend, donning hanfu not only in historical lanes but in everyday life, and weaving those visuals into the fabric of social media. Our reporter Ioana Gomoi meets the creators who are turning heritage into a trendy cultural inspiration.

programme.title: Tales in Light and Shadow
programme.desc: Beijing shadow puppets bring ancient Chinese tales to life with intricately crafted leather figures. Behind an illuminated screen, these colorful silhouettes dance, enacting epic stories and folklore accompanied by traditional music. Discover this captivating performing art, a cherished part of China's rich cultural tapestry and a mesmerizing visual spectacle.

programme.title: LIVING THE NEW WAVE China's livestream shopping boom redefines e-commerce
programme.desc: Now, we’re taking you inside one of China’s most dynamic phenomena—the livestream shopping revolution. From the misty tea mountains of Guizhou to Beijing’s glittering jewelry studios, millions tune in daily not just to purchase, but to connect. How did this fusion of entertainment and e-commerce become such a powerful cultural force? And what does it tell us about the future of shopping worldwide? Our reporter Lucy Lyv is here with us.

programme.title: Taohuawu New Year's Painting
programme.desc: Taohuawu New Year woodblock prints, originating from Suzhou, are iconic symbols of Chinese folk art. These vibrant prints, traditionally displayed during Chinese New Year, depict auspicious scenes, deities, and daily life, embodying wishes for prosperity and happiness. Explore the intricate carving and printing techniques that bring these colorful cultural treasures to life.

programme.title: INVESTING IN JOY: Inside China's new emotional economy
programme.desc: Today's young Chinese generation are driving a new \"emotional economy,\" spending not just on necessities but on feelings. From trendy toys that offer joy and community, to serene scents that provide a moment of peace – they are investing in personal well-being, reshaping the consumer landscape.

programme.title: A Sip of Mount Wuyi
programme.desc: Wuyi rock tea, nurtured in the mineral-rich cliffs of the Wuyi Mountains, is a distinctive oolong embodying a unique \"rock rhyme\" (yan yun)—a taste of nature's harmony. More than a beverage, teas like Da Hong Pao invite mindful appreciation, reflecting centuries of tradition and a philosophy of patience in their complex roasted notes and enduring aftertaste. Discover the terroir, meticulous processing, and the spirit of Chinese tea culture within these exceptional leaves.

programme.title: The Inner Horizon: China's extreme sports revolution
programme.desc: They say in China, the most exciting frontiers aren't always on a map. A new cultural wave is sweeping the nation, turning vertical rock faces, open skies, and dense forests into new playgrounds. What was once the domain of a handful of adventurers is now a booming lifestyle for millions of young Chinese. I recently ventured out to explore why extreme sports are becoming the new national pastime, and what this reveals about the changing spirit of China.

programme.title: A Lifelong Journey of Xuan Paper
programme.desc: The host travels to Jingxian, Anhui—known as the hometown of Xuan paper—to experience the intricate process of making this traditional paper. Along the way, they uncover the secrets behind its thousand-year legacy and witness the craftsmanship of Xuan paper inheritors at every stage. Through vivid, personal stories, the episode reveals the hidden struggles and quiet perseverance that have kept this intangible cultural heritage alive across generations.

programme.title: The beat of the city: Inside China's booming Livehouse scene
programme.desc: From the intimate jazz vibes of Beijing's Blue Note to the electric energy of a Guangzhou livehouse — we dive into China's booming nightlife scene. Discover how these venues are more than just places for a night out — they're cultural hubs where young audiences connect, artists innovate, and the city's heart beats to a brand new rhythm.

programme.title: All About the Yak
programme.desc: In this episode, we go to Nangqian, Yushu, Qinghai Province, to get close contact with the Yak in Tibet, which is known as the \" Vehicle on the Plateau\". During the bustling yak culture festival, people gather from near and far, meadows are crowded, and yak appraisal activities are also in full swing. As the journey continues, we are able to understand several aspects of the yak culture which is closely related to the life of Tibetan people.

programme.title: Dive into the world of foreign talent in China's movie industry
programme.desc: We go behind the scenes of China's fast-evolving film industry with foreign actors making their mark here. From script study to on-set cultural moments, they share how they learn, interpret, and help bring Chinese stories to life. Their journeys highlight not only China's growing appeal and openness but also the vibrant, two-way cultural exchange unfolding on the global stage.

programme.title: The Yak and the Nomad
programme.desc: Qinghai’s Yushu Prefecture is one of China’s key yak-producing regions. This trip took us to Nangqian County, where we explored the yak industry that lies at the heart of Tibetan herders’ daily lives. Trekking into the far-flung pastoral corners of the plateau, we listened closely to the stories behind the hurdles and hopes of pastoral life and development.

programme.title: Personalized tours draw more global travelers to Beijing
programme.desc: As China’s visa-free policy continues to attract visitors from around the world, more international tourists are choosing customized experiences in Beijing. Local guides fluent in multiple languages are helping them discover the city in their own way. Reporter Li Qiong joined oneone of the capital’s most popular guides to see how personalized tours are reshaping travel in China.

programme.title: China Bound: Tianjin
programme.desc: Tianran He explores the vibrant port city of Tianjin. Strolling through its historic quarters, he discovers how old buildings are being preserved and revitalized, and savors the rich flavors of authentic Tianjin cuisine. To work off the calories, he joins energetic retirees for a morning workout in the park, then takes in the lively nightlife along the banks of the Haihe River. From folk handicrafts on Ancient Culture Street to the National Maritime Museum and an aircraft carrier theme park, Tianran discovers that Tianjin is a truly multifacted city.

programme.title: From plush toys to holograms: The growing world of AI companionship
programme.desc: Artificial intelligence is evolving—from answering our questions to keeping us company. From robotic pets in our neighborhoods to holographic characters that sense our mood, AI companionship is stepping out of science fiction and into everyday life. What does this mean for human relationships and the future of connection itself? Reporter Zhang Hong explores the rise of these new companions.

programme.title: China Highly Recommended: Changsha
programme.desc: Episode 1 of Travelogue's \"China Highly Recommended\" tells the story of Changsha, one of China's happiest cities. Following the lives of four of its foreign residents: A French executive chef, a Japanese podcaster, and two American teachers, we discover the reasons why they made Changsha their second home, and explore the city with them as only locals do. This episode will be released as a 30 minute travel documentary to be broadcast on CGTN's television channel and social media platform, along with a series of video clips and photos.

programme.title: Young Chinese dare to express their culture
programme.desc: A fresh chapter in cultural storytelling is being written by young Chinese around the world. Young Chinese are amplifying tradition through trend, donning hanfu not only in historical lanes but in everyday life, and weaving those visuals into the fabric of social media. Our reporter Ioana Gomoi meets the creators who are turning heritage into a trendy cultural inspiration.

programme.title: Tales in Light and Shadow
programme.desc: Beijing shadow puppets bring ancient Chinese tales to life with intricately crafted leather figures. Behind an illuminated screen, these colorful silhouettes dance, enacting epic stories and folklore accompanied by traditional music. Discover this captivating performing art, a cherished part of China's rich cultural tapestry and a mesmerizing visual spectacle.

programme.title: LIVING THE NEW WAVE China's livestream shopping boom redefines e-commerce
programme.desc: Now, we’re taking you inside one of China’s most dynamic phenomena—the livestream shopping revolution. From the misty tea mountains of Guizhou to Beijing’s glittering jewelry studios, millions tune in daily not just to purchase, but to connect. How did this fusion of entertainment and e-commerce become such a powerful cultural force? And what does it tell us about the future of shopping worldwide? Our reporter Lucy Lyv is here with us.

programme.title: Taohuawu New Year's Painting
programme.desc: Taohuawu New Year woodblock prints, originating from Suzhou, are iconic symbols of Chinese folk art. These vibrant prints, traditionally displayed during Chinese New Year, depict auspicious scenes, deities, and daily life, embodying wishes for prosperity and happiness. Explore the intricate carving and printing techniques that bring these colorful cultural treasures to life.

programme.title: INVESTING IN JOY: Inside China's new emotional economy
programme.desc: Today's young Chinese generation are driving a new \"emotional economy,\" spending not just on necessities but on feelings. From trendy toys that offer joy and community, to serene scents that provide a moment of peace – they are investing in personal well-being, reshaping the consumer landscape.

programme.title: A Sip of Mount Wuyi
programme.desc: Wuyi rock tea, nurtured in the mineral-rich cliffs of the Wuyi Mountains, is a distinctive oolong embodying a unique \"rock rhyme\" (yan yun)—a taste of nature's harmony. More than a beverage, teas like Da Hong Pao invite mindful appreciation, reflecting centuries of tradition and a philosophy of patience in their complex roasted notes and enduring aftertaste. Discover the terroir, meticulous processing, and the spirit of Chinese tea culture within these exceptional leaves.

programme.title: The Inner Horizon: China's extreme sports revolution
programme.desc: They say in China, the most exciting frontiers aren't always on a map. A new cultural wave is sweeping the nation, turning vertical rock faces, open skies, and dense forests into new playgrounds. What was once the domain of a handful of adventurers is now a booming lifestyle for millions of young Chinese. I recently ventured out to explore why extreme sports are becoming the new national pastime, and what this reveals about the changing spirit of China.

programme.title: A Lifelong Journey of Xuan Paper
programme.desc: The host travels to Jingxian, Anhui—known as the hometown of Xuan paper—to experience the intricate process of making this traditional paper. Along the way, they uncover the secrets behind its thousand-year legacy and witness the craftsmanship of Xuan paper inheritors at every stage. Through vivid, personal stories, the episode reveals the hidden struggles and quiet perseverance that have kept this intangible cultural heritage alive across generations.

programme.title: The beat of the city: Inside China's booming Livehouse scene
programme.desc: From the intimate jazz vibes of Beijing's Blue Note to the electric energy of a Guangzhou livehouse — we dive into China's booming nightlife scene. Discover how these venues are more than just places for a night out — they're cultural hubs where young audiences connect, artists innovate, and the city's heart beats to a brand new rhythm.

programme.title: All About the Yak
programme.desc: In this episode, we go to Nangqian, Yushu, Qinghai Province, to get close contact with the Yak in Tibet, which is known as the \" Vehicle on the Plateau\". During the bustling yak culture festival, people gather from near and far, meadows are crowded, and yak appraisal activities are also in full swing. As the journey continues, we are able to understand several aspects of the yak culture which is closely related to the life of Tibetan people.

programme.title: Dive into the world of foreign talent in China's movie industry
programme.desc: We go behind the scenes of China's fast-evolving film industry with foreign actors making their mark here. From script study to on-set cultural moments, they share how they learn, interpret, and help bring Chinese stories to life. Their journeys highlight not only China's growing appeal and openness but also the vibrant, two-way cultural exchange unfolding on the global stage.

programme.title: The Yak and the Nomad
programme.desc: Qinghai’s Yushu Prefecture is one of China’s key yak-producing regions. This trip took us to Nangqian County, where we explored the yak industry that lies at the heart of Tibetan herders’ daily lives. Trekking into the far-flung pastoral corners of the plateau, we listened closely to the stories behind the hurdles and hopes of pastoral life and development.

programme.title: Personalized tours draw more global travelers to Beijing
programme.desc: As China’s visa-free policy continues to attract visitors from around the world, more international tourists are choosing customized experiences in Beijing. Local guides fluent in multiple languages are helping them discover the city in their own way. Reporter Li Qiong joined oneone of the capital’s most popular guides to see how personalized tours are reshaping travel in China.

programme.title: China Bound: Tianjin
programme.desc: Tianran He explores the vibrant port city of Tianjin. Strolling through its historic quarters, he discovers how old buildings are being preserved and revitalized, and savors the rich flavors of authentic Tianjin cuisine. To work off the calories, he joins energetic retirees for a morning workout in the park, then takes in the lively nightlife along the banks of the Haihe River. From folk handicrafts on Ancient Culture Street to the National Maritime Museum and an aircraft carrier theme park, Tianran discovers that Tianjin is a truly multifacted city.

programme.title: From plush toys to holograms: The growing world of AI companionship
programme.desc: Artificial intelligence is evolving—from answering our questions to keeping us company. From robotic pets in our neighborhoods to holographic characters that sense our mood, AI companionship is stepping out of science fiction and into everyday life. What does this mean for human relationships and the future of connection itself? Reporter Zhang Hong explores the rise of these new companions.

programme.title: China Highly Recommended: Changsha
programme.desc: Episode 1 of Travelogue's \"China Highly Recommended\" tells the story of Changsha, one of China's happiest cities. Following the lives of four of its foreign residents: A French executive chef, a Japanese podcaster, and two American teachers, we discover the reasons why they made Changsha their second home, and explore the city with them as only locals do. This episode will be released as a 30 minute travel documentary to be broadcast on CGTN's television channel and social media platform, along with a series of video clips and photos.

programme.title: Young Chinese dare to express their culture
programme.desc: A fresh chapter in cultural storytelling is being written by young Chinese around the world. Young Chinese are amplifying tradition through trend, donning hanfu not only in historical lanes but in everyday life, and weaving those visuals into the fabric of social media. Our reporter Ioana Gomoi meets the creators who are turning heritage into a trendy cultural inspiration.

programme.title: Tales in Light and Shadow
programme.desc: Beijing shadow puppets bring ancient Chinese tales to life with intricately crafted leather figures. Behind an illuminated screen, these colorful silhouettes dance, enacting epic stories and folklore accompanied by traditional music. Discover this captivating performing art, a cherished part of China's rich cultural tapestry and a mesmerizing visual spectacle.

programme.title: LIVING THE NEW WAVE China's livestream shopping boom redefines e-commerce
programme.desc: Now, we’re taking you inside one of China’s most dynamic phenomena—the livestream shopping revolution. From the misty tea mountains of Guizhou to Beijing’s glittering jewelry studios, millions tune in daily not just to purchase, but to connect. How did this fusion of entertainment and e-commerce become such a powerful cultural force? And what does it tell us about the future of shopping worldwide? Our reporter Lucy Lyv is here with us.

programme.title: Taohuawu New Year's Painting
programme.desc: Taohuawu New Year woodblock prints, originating from Suzhou, are iconic symbols of Chinese folk art. These vibrant prints, traditionally displayed during Chinese New Year, depict auspicious scenes, deities, and daily life, embodying wishes for prosperity and happiness. Explore the intricate carving and printing techniques that bring these colorful cultural treasures to life.

programme.title: INVESTING IN JOY: Inside China's new emotional economy
programme.desc: Today's young Chinese generation are driving a new \"emotional economy,\" spending not just on necessities but on feelings. From trendy toys that offer joy and community, to serene scents that provide a moment of peace – they are investing in personal well-being, reshaping the consumer landscape.

programme.title: A Sip of Mount Wuyi
programme.desc: Wuyi rock tea, nurtured in the mineral-rich cliffs of the Wuyi Mountains, is a distinctive oolong embodying a unique \"rock rhyme\" (yan yun)—a taste of nature's harmony. More than a beverage, teas like Da Hong Pao invite mindful appreciation, reflecting centuries of tradition and a philosophy of patience in their complex roasted notes and enduring aftertaste. Discover the terroir, meticulous processing, and the spirit of Chinese tea culture within these exceptional leaves.

programme.title: The Inner Horizon: China's extreme sports revolution
programme.desc: They say in China, the most exciting frontiers aren't always on a map. A new cultural wave is sweeping the nation, turning vertical rock faces, open skies, and dense forests into new playgrounds. What was once the domain of a handful of adventurers is now a booming lifestyle for millions of young Chinese. I recently ventured out to explore why extreme sports are becoming the new national pastime, and what this reveals about the changing spirit of China.

programme.title: A Lifelong Journey of Xuan Paper
programme.desc: The host travels to Jingxian, Anhui—known as the hometown of Xuan paper—to experience the intricate process of making this traditional paper. Along the way, they uncover the secrets behind its thousand-year legacy and witness the craftsmanship of Xuan paper inheritors at every stage. Through vivid, personal stories, the episode reveals the hidden struggles and quiet perseverance that have kept this intangible cultural heritage alive across generations.

programme.title: The beat of the city: Inside China's booming Livehouse scene
programme.desc: From the intimate jazz vibes of Beijing's Blue Note to the electric energy of a Guangzhou livehouse — we dive into China's booming nightlife scene. Discover how these venues are more than just places for a night out — they're cultural hubs where young audiences connect, artists innovate, and the city's heart beats to a brand new rhythm.

programme.title: China Untapped: Nanjing 1
programme.desc: Nanjing, an ancient city that served as the capital of six imperial dynasties, stands as a testament to China's long-standing history and rich culture. From its Porcelain Tower - one of the Seven Wonders of the Middle Ages, to its grand city walls and ancient palaces, join us on a voyage across time to experience the echoes of history in Nanjing's storied bricks.

programme.title: Dive into the world of foreign talent in China's movie industry
programme.desc: We go behind the scenes of China's fast-evolving film industry with foreign actors making their mark here. From script study to on-set cultural moments, they share how they learn, interpret, and help bring Chinese stories to life. Their journeys highlight not only China's growing appeal and openness but also the vibrant, two-way cultural exchange unfolding on the global stage.

programme.title: China Untapped: Nanjing 2
programme.desc: In this episode, we explore the grand old mansions of Nanjing’s merchant families and take a stroll through their exquisite gardens, where Nature is incorporated into the architecture. From centuries-old Qing Dynasty residences to traditional folk handicrafts, join us on a deep dive into Nanjing’s rich cultural heritage.

programme.title: Personalized tours draw more global travelers to Beijing
programme.desc: As China’s visa-free policy continues to attract visitors from around the world, more international tourists are choosing customized experiences in Beijing. Local guides fluent in multiple languages are helping them discover the city in their own way. Reporter Li Qiong joined oneone of the capital’s most popular guides to see how personalized tours are reshaping travel in China.

programme.title: China Untapped: Nanjing 3
programme.desc: Join us in Nanjing’s bustling old quarter, where you can try on silk dresses worth their weight in gold, and see how traditional handicrafts have been given modern twists. We’ll feast on street snacks and local delicacies by the old Confucian Temple, before taking a pleasure cruise along Nanjing’s mother river to round off our exploration of this vibrant and historic city.

programme.title: From plush toys to holograms: The growing world of AI companionship
programme.desc: Artificial intelligence is evolving—from answering our questions to keeping us company. From robotic pets in our neighborhoods to holographic characters that sense our mood, AI companionship is stepping out of science fiction and into everyday life. What does this mean for human relationships and the future of connection itself? Reporter Zhang Hong explores the rise of these new companions.

programme.title: Nyingchi Ep.1
programme.desc: Join Tianran He in Nyingchi, Xizang Autonomous Region. Fed by the Yarlung Zangbo River and abundant rainfall, this lush subtropical region is home to some of Xizan’g most unique scenery. From sacred mountains and ancient monasteries, to cruising along Nyingchi’s mother river, wandering alpine meadows and savoring stone pot chicken – a beloved local delicacy, Tianran uncovers the beauty of Nyingchi.

programme.title: Young Chinese dare to express their culture
programme.desc: A fresh chapter in cultural storytelling is being written by young Chinese around the world. Young Chinese are amplifying tradition through trend, donning hanfu not only in historical lanes but in everyday life, and weaving those visuals into the fabric of social media. Our reporter Ioana Gomoi meets the creators who are turning heritage into a trendy cultural inspiration.

programme.title: Dance of Heroes
programme.desc: In this episode, our host “Uncle Hanzi” travels to Chaonan in Shantou to explore the story of a family devoted to the tradition of Yingge dance. Centered around inheritor Hong Shaohua, the story unfolds through his father’s dedication, his daughter’s passion, and his apprentices’ enthusiasm—showcasing how this centuries-old folk art continues to thrive in the modern world.

programme.title: LIVING THE NEW WAVE China's livestream shopping boom redefines e-commerce
programme.desc: Now, we’re taking you inside one of China’s most dynamic phenomena—the livestream shopping revolution. From the misty tea mountains of Guizhou to Beijing’s glittering jewelry studios, millions tune in daily not just to purchase, but to connect. How did this fusion of entertainment and e-commerce become such a powerful cultural force? And what does it tell us about the future of shopping worldwide? Our reporter Lucy Lyv is here with us.

programme.title: Living Arts of Huizhou
programme.desc: This is Huangshan in eastern China. In this city, you’ll encounter one of China’s most traditional building styles: Hui-style architecture. How does Hui style set this region apart? What’s life like for people living in these houses? With these questions in mind, we’re embarking on a journey to decode Hui-style architecture.

programme.title: INVESTING IN JOY: Inside China's new emotional economy
programme.desc: Today's young Chinese generation are driving a new \"emotional economy,\" spending not just on necessities but on feelings. From trendy toys that offer joy and community, to serene scents that provide a moment of peace – they are investing in personal well-being, reshaping the consumer landscape.

programme.title: Pingyao Hand-Polished Lacquer Art
programme.desc: Pingyao is an old town with a history of over 2,700 years in the north of China, in Shanxi Province. The rich cultural heritage it built up over the centuries eventually gave rise to a very special art—Tuiguang lacquerware. This hand-polished lacquerware, unique to Pingyao, is recognized as one of the four representative lacquerwares of China.

programme.title: The Inner Horizon: China's extreme sports revolution
programme.desc: They say in China, the most exciting frontiers aren't always on a map. A new cultural wave is sweeping the nation, turning vertical rock faces, open skies, and dense forests into new playgrounds. What was once the domain of a handful of adventurers is now a booming lifestyle for millions of young Chinese. I recently ventured out to explore why extreme sports are becoming the new national pastime, and what this reveals about the changing spirit of China.

programme.title: Suzhou Fans
programme.desc: Suzhou, known as the “Venice of China,” is also famous for its traditional crafts. And one of the most elegant and exquisite crafts is Suzhou fans. Celebrated for their exquisite design and fine quality, they’re popularly associated with literati. How come Suzhou gave birth to such a variety of fans? How can we appreciate their beauty in the modern world? In this episode, we are discovering more stories behind Suzhou fans.

programme.title: The beat of the city: Inside China's booming Livehouse scene
programme.desc: From the intimate jazz vibes of Beijing's Blue Note to the electric energy of a Guangzhou livehouse — we dive into China's booming nightlife scene. Discover how these venues are more than just places for a night out — they're cultural hubs where young audiences connect, artists innovate, and the city's heart beats to a brand new rhythm.

programme.title: Miao Silverware: A Shining Epic
programme.desc: In this episode, we explore the fascinating world of the silver-working art of the Miao ethnic group. Join us, and hear the stories and see the works of four remarkable individuals. From a national-level inheritor who is preserving the traditional craft, to an artist infusing personal flair into his designs; from a female silversmith imbuing her work with feminine warmth, to a young artisan inspiring a new generation, we investigate the rich heritage, cultural significance, and evolving potential of Miao silverware.

programme.title: China Untapped: Nanjing 1
programme.desc: Nanjing, an ancient city that served as the capital of six imperial dynasties, stands as a testament to China's long-standing history and rich culture. From its Porcelain Tower - one of the Seven Wonders of the Middle Ages, to its grand city walls and ancient palaces, join us on a voyage across time to experience the echoes of history in Nanjing's storied bricks.

programme.title: Dive into the world of foreign talent in China's movie industry
programme.desc: We go behind the scenes of China's fast-evolving film industry with foreign actors making their mark here. From script study to on-set cultural moments, they share how they learn, interpret, and help bring Chinese stories to life. Their journeys highlight not only China's growing appeal and openness but also the vibrant, two-way cultural exchange unfolding on the global stage.

programme.title: China Untapped: Nanjing 2
programme.desc: In this episode, we explore the grand old mansions of Nanjing’s merchant families and take a stroll through their exquisite gardens, where Nature is incorporated into the architecture. From centuries-old Qing Dynasty residences to traditional folk handicrafts, join us on a deep dive into Nanjing’s rich cultural heritage.

programme.title: Personalized tours draw more global travelers to Beijing
programme.desc: As China’s visa-free policy continues to attract visitors from around the world, more international tourists are choosing customized experiences in Beijing. Local guides fluent in multiple languages are helping them discover the city in their own way. Reporter Li Qiong joined oneone of the capital’s most popular guides to see how personalized tours are reshaping travel in China.

programme.title: China Untapped: Nanjing 3
programme.desc: Join us in Nanjing’s bustling old quarter, where you can try on silk dresses worth their weight in gold, and see how traditional handicrafts have been given modern twists. We’ll feast on street snacks and local delicacies by the old Confucian Temple, before taking a pleasure cruise along Nanjing’s mother river to round off our exploration of this vibrant and historic city.

programme.title: From plush toys to holograms: The growing world of AI companionship
programme.desc: Artificial intelligence is evolving—from answering our questions to keeping us company. From robotic pets in our neighborhoods to holographic characters that sense our mood, AI companionship is stepping out of science fiction and into everyday life. What does this mean for human relationships and the future of connection itself? Reporter Zhang Hong explores the rise of these new companions.
